title: Modals of Deduction
description: MUST BE / CAN'T BE / MIGHT BE express how certain you are about something based on evidence.
summary_pt_br: Modais de dedução: MUST BE (certeza positiva), MIGHT BE (talvez), CAN'T BE (certeza negativa). Passado: must have been.

scenes.subtitle_en: MUST BE / CAN'T BE / MIGHT BE express how certain you are about something based on evidence.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Modais de dedução: MUST BE (certeza positiva), MIGHT BE (talvez), CAN'T BE (certeza negativa). Passado: must have been.

scenes.text_en: MUST BE / CAN'T BE / MIGHT BE express how certain you are about something based on evidence. Today's focus: modals of deduction. The lights are on — someone must be home.
scenes.text_pt: Modais de dedução: MUST BE (certeza positiva), MIGHT BE (talvez), CAN'T BE (certeza negativa). Passado: must have been. Foco de hoje: modals of deduction. As luzes estão acesas — alguém deve estar em casa.

scenes.title: Modals of Deduction
scenes.body_en: MUST BE = I am 95% sure. MIGHT / COULD / MAY BE = maybe (50%). CAN'T BE = I am 95% sure not. For past: must HAVE been, might HAVE been, can't HAVE been.
scenes.body_pt: Modais de dedução: MUST BE (certeza positiva), MIGHT BE (talvez), CAN'T BE (certeza negativa). Passado: must have been.

examples.en: The lights are on — someone must be home.
examples.pt: As luzes estão acesas — alguém deve estar em casa.

examples.en: She might be at the library. I'm not sure.
examples.pt: Ela pode estar na biblioteca. Não tenho certeza.

examples.en: That can't be his car — he sold it last week.
examples.pt: Esse não pode ser o carro dele — vendeu semana passada.

exercise.question: Complete: "The lights are on — ___ must be home."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "The lights are on — someone must be home."
exercise.hint_pt_br: As luzes estão acesas — alguém deve estar em casa.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
She might be at the library. I'm not sure.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "She might be at the library. I'm not sure."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ela pode estar na biblioteca. Não tenho certeza.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Modals of Deduction — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre modals of deduction. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Look at the footprints in the garden. Whose can they be?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: They must be the gardener's — he comes on Mondays.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: But these are much smaller than his shoes.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Then they might be my sister's. She walks here.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: She is in school right now, so it can't be her.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It could be a neighbor who took a shortcut.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: The gate was locked. How would they have entered?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Someone must have jumped over the fence.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: That can't be right. The fence is two meters tall.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Then it might be someone from inside the house.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Now we are getting somewhere. Good thinking, Maya.

scenes.title: The mystery in the garden
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe the clues and write what the speakers deduce. Use at least two modals of deduction.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In the quiet garden behind the school, Maya noticed something strange in the morning dew.

paragraphs.speaker: Maya
paragraphs.en: These footprints must belong to someone small, maybe a child or a teenager.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: But children can't have entered. The gate was locked all night.

paragraphs.speaker: Maya
paragraphs.en: Then the person might have come from inside the school itself.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: That could be the new student who arrived yesterday. She was exploring.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Mystery solved — and another small lesson in the power of careful observation.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Modals of Deduction". MUST BE / CAN'T BE / MIGHT BE express how certain you are about something based on evidence. The lights are on — someone must be home. She might be at the library. I'm not sure. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Modals of Deduction". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Modals of Deduction".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
